title: Ouratea revoluta
taxonomy: Ouratea revoluta is classified as a species within the genus Ouratea, which is part of the family Ochnaceae. The Ochnaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the polypetalous family. Ouratea revoluta is native to tropical regions and is a part of the tropical and subtropical rainforests. The plant is a shrub or small tree, with various characteristics including its leaves and flowers. Its classification also includes its family membership within the order Malpighiales.

intro: Ouratea revoluta is a plant species belonging to the family Ochthidaceae, commonly known as the Ouratea or Bastardia tree. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents found in Ouratea revoluta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ouratea revoluta is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues.
